01_initial_request.input_data: Wild-type GmASNase sequence and homologous protein sequences for consensus analysis
01_initial_request.user_intent: To engineer GmASNase with enhanced thermal stability for efficient acrylamide mitigation in thermal food processing
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Increased half-life at 50°C and 55°C and increased melting temperature (Tm) compared to wild-type

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Wild-type GmASNase sequence was available from previous study (Chi et al. 2021). [Gap] Need to identify conserved residues that could improve thermostability through consensus analysis. [Decision] To achieve thermostability enhancement, chose to use Consensus Finder online server to perform consensus sequence analysis on GmASNase, expecting to obtain a list of potential stable consensus mutations.

parameters.input_sequence: GmASNase amino acid sequence (accession number WP_069434439)
parameters.exclusion_criteria: substitution sites within 6-Å distance from active site (Thr12, Thr84) excluded

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 17 residues selected for further study: Ala172, Ala203, Ala286, Val56, Arg134, Phe44, Thr34, Ile71, Val163, Met178, Val185, Val230, Gly97, Asn159, Lys135, Glu249, and Gly164

references.relevance: Consensus Finder web tool to predict stabilizing substitutions in proteins

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 17 candidate residues identified from consensus analysis. [Gap] Need to create mutation libraries for each residue to test thermostability improvements. [Decision] To achieve mutant library construction, chose to use 2× Phanta Flash Master Mix to perform whole plasmid PCR-based site-directed saturation mutagenesis on pET30a-GmASNase template, expecting to obtain random mutagenesis libraries for all 17 residues.

parameters.digestion: 2 μL QuickCut DpnI for 2 h on methylated or hemimethylated parental templates

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Random mutagenesis libraries prepared for all 17 selected residues using degenerate primers

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Mutation libraries constructed for 17 residues. [Gap] Need to screen mutants for improved thermal stability. [Decision] To achieve mutant screening, chose to use high-throughput screening method with thermal treatment to perform activity comparison between ice-treated and heat-treated samples, expecting to identify mutants with higher residual activity after heat treatment.

parameters.selection_criteria: higher residual activity than control in heat-treated plate compared to ice-treated plate

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Mutants with improved thermostability identified including N159H, N159D, V230M, N159W, A172G, G164E, R134E, E249Q, V163C, and G97T

references.relevance: High-throughput screening method for enzyme mutants

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Multiple single mutants identified from screening. [Gap] Need to characterize specific activities and half-lives of promising mutants. [Decision] To achieve mutant characterization, chose to use Nessler method to perform ASNase activity assay and thermal stability measurement, expecting to obtain specific activity and half-life values for each mutant.

parameters.measurement: OD436 after 5 min with Nessler's reagent

02_agent_trajectory.observation: G97T showed greatest improvement with half-life of 990.21±5.99 min at 40°C (29.57 times wild-type); N159H, N159D, V230M showed 1.62, 1.40, 0.75 times higher specific activity than wild-type

references.relevance: Nessler method for ASNase activity measurement

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] G97T identified as best single mutant with 29.57x half-life improvement. [Gap] Need to combine G97T with other beneficial mutations for further improvement. [Decision] To achieve combinatorial mutagenesis, chose to use site-directed mutagenesis to perform random mutations on residues Asn159, Val230, Ala172, Arg134, Glu249, and Val163 using G97T as template, expecting to obtain double mutants with enhanced thermostability.

parameters.target_residues: Asn159, Val230, Ala172, Arg134, Glu249, Val163

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Double mutants generated including G97T/V230A, G97T/V230P, G97T/V230Q, G97T/V163C, G97T/R134P, G97T/R134D, G97T/N159H, G97T/N159F, G97T/N159C, G97T/N159W, G97T/N159Y

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Double mutant library constructed based on G97T template. [Gap] Need to screen double mutants for best thermostability and activity combination. [Decision] To achieve double mutant selection, chose to use thermal stability and activity assay to perform comparative analysis at 45°C, expecting to identify best double mutant for further engineering.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: G97T/N159Y (TY) identified as best double mutant with half-life 16.43-fold higher than G97T and 135.03-fold higher than wild-type at 45°C, with slightly improved specific activity

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] TY (G97T/N159Y) identified as best double mutant. [Gap] Need to further improve thermostability by adding third mutation. [Decision] To achieve triple mutant generation, chose to use random mutagenesis to perform mutations on residues Val230, Ala172, Arg134, Glu249, and Val163 using TY as template, expecting to obtain triple mutants with enhanced thermostability and activity.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Triple mutants generated including TY/V230T, TY/V230L, TY/V230M, TY/E249Q, TY/R134S

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Triple mutant library constructed based on TY template. [Gap] Need to identify best triple mutant with optimal thermostability and activity. [Decision] To achieve triple mutant selection, chose to use thermal stability and activity assay at 50°C to perform comparative analysis, expecting to identify best triple mutant for comprehensive characterization.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: TY/E249Q (TYQ) identified as best triple mutant with 5.46-fold higher half-life than TY at 50°C and 1.28 times higher specific activity; Gly97, Asn159, and Glu249 identified as key residues

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Triple mutant TYQ (G97T/N159Y/E249Q) identified as best candidate. [Gap] Need to comprehensively characterize thermal stability across multiple temperatures. [Decision] To achieve thermal stability profiling, chose to use half-life measurement method to perform incubation at 40, 45, 50, and 55°C with first-order linear regression analysis, expecting to obtain t1/2 values at each temperature.

parameters.calculation: t1/2 = ln2/kd where kd determined by first-order linear regression: ln A = kd × t

02_agent_trajectory.observation: TYQ showed t1/2 of 61.65±8.69 min at 50°C and 5.12±1.66 min at 55°C, while wild-type was completely inactive at these conditions; TYQ maintained >206.25 times higher t1/2 than wild-type at 45°C

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Half-life values determined for TYQ and wild-type. [Gap] Need to measure melting temperature to confirm structural stability improvement. [Decision] To achieve Tm measurement, chose to use Protein Thermal Shift Dye Kit to perform fluorescence monitoring with StepOne real-time PCR system, expecting to obtain Tm values for wild-type and mutants.

parameters.kit: Protein Thermal Shift™ Dye Kit (Thermo-Fisher Scientific)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: TYQ Tm value of 67.42°C, increased by 8.59°C from wild-type (58.83±0.21°C)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Thermal stability parameters (t1/2 and Tm) determined. [Gap] Need to characterize enzymatic properties for industrial application assessment. [Decision] To achieve enzymatic property characterization, chose to use activity assay to perform optimum temperature and pH determination with relative activity calculation, expecting to obtain optimal reaction conditions for TYQ.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: TYQ displayed optimum reaction temperature of 50°C (same as wild-type) and optimum pH of 9.0; TYQ showed significantly wider pH range than wild-type and other mutants

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Optimum temperature and pH determined for TYQ. [Gap] Need to determine kinetic parameters for catalytic efficiency comparison. [Decision] To achieve kinetic parameter determination, chose to use Michaelis-Menten analysis to perform non-linear regression fitting with L-ASN substrate at 1-10 mM, expecting to obtain Km, kcat, and kcat/Km values.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: TYQ displayed slightly higher catalytic efficiency than wild-type and highest specific activity among wild-type and other mutants; G97T had lowest Km value indicating better substrate binding

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Experimental characterization of TYQ completed. [Gap] Need to build 3D structure model for structural analysis of mutations. [Decision] To achieve structural modeling, chose to use Swiss-Model to perform homology modeling using H. pylori ASNase crystal structure as template, expecting to obtain 3D structure models of wild-type and mutants.

parameters.template: crystal structure of ASNase from Helicobacter pylori (PDB: 2wlt)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 3D structure models built for wild-type and mutant TYQ

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 3D structure models generated for wild-type and TYQ. [Gap] Need to analyze residue interactions and surface charge distributions around mutation sites. [Decision] To achieve structural interaction analysis, chose to use PyMOL software to perform interaction analysis of mutant residues with other residues, expecting to identify hydrogen bonds, hydrophobic interactions, and electrostatic potential changes.

parameters.analysis_type: interaction of mutant residues with other residues

02_agent_trajectory.observation: G97T formed additional hydrogen bonds with Leu94 and Trp93; N159Y formed increased hydrophobic interactions with surrounding residues; E249Q showed favorable electrostatic potential distribution consistent with surrounding potential

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Static structural analysis completed. [Gap] Need to investigate dynamic flexibility and stability through molecular dynamics simulations. [Decision] To achieve MD simulation analysis, chose to use AMBER 18 software to perform all-atom molecular dynamics simulations on dimeric complexes, expecting to obtain RMSD, RMSF, Rg, and SASA values for stability comparison.

parameters.solvent: truncated octahedral TIP3P solvent box at 10Å distance
parameters.ions: Na+ and Cl- for charge balancing
parameters.equilibration: 200-ps ramp from 0 to 330K, 500-ps NVT, 50-ns NPT equilibrium

02_agent_trajectory.observation: TYQ showed lower RMSD (1.50Å vs 1.60Å), lower Rg (25.29Å vs 25.42Å), and lower SASA (149.91Å²) than wild-type; TYQ had significantly smaller fluctuations in loops 157-166 (chain A) and loops 26-34, 185-196, 240-250 (chain B)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] MD simulation data obtained showing TYQ has more stable structure. [Gap] Need to correlate structural changes with thermostability mechanism. [Decision] To achieve mechanism elucidation, chose to use structural analysis to perform correlation of hydrogen bonds, hydrophobic interactions, and electrostatic potential with thermal stability data, expecting to reveal molecular basis for TYQ thermostability enhancement.

parameters.factors_analyzed: hydrogen bonds, hydrophobic interactions, electrostatic potential

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Additional hydrogen bonds (G97T), increased hydrophobic interactions (N159Y), and favorable electrostatic potential (E249Q) were essential for TYQ being in more rigid state for thermostability enhancement

references.relevance: Hydrophobic interactions contributed approximately 64% to protein stability
Symmetry of electrostatic potential on protein surface affects thermal stability

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Thermal stability measurement (half-life at multiple temperatures and Tm determination) combined with enzymatic activity assay and molecular dynamics simulation

half_life_50C.interpretation: TYQ maintained activity at 50°C while wild-type was completely inactive

half_life_55C.interpretation: TYQ maintained activity at 55°C while wild-type was completely inactive

half_life_45C_improvement.interpretation: TYQ maintained more than 206.25 times higher half-life than wild-type at 45°C

Tm_TYQ.interpretation: Final melting temperature of engineered triple mutant TYQ

catalytic_efficiency.interpretation: TYQ displayed slightly higher catalytic efficiency (kcat/Km) than wild-type while maintaining excellent specific activity

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved. The engineered triple mutant TYQ (G97T/N159Y/E249Q) successfully demonstrated significantly enhanced thermal stability with increased half-life at 50°C (61.65±8.69 min vs inactive wild-type) and 55°C (5.12±1.66 min vs inactive wild-type), and increased Tm by 8.59°C (67.42°C vs 58.83°C). The mutant also maintained excellent catalytic efficiency and specific activity, making it a potential agent for efficient acrylamide mitigation in food industry thermal processing.
